heading: Use of certain appropriated funds
label: 903 Use of certain appropriated funds
notes: Codificationamended subsec. (d)(1) of this section “amended by section 241(b)(1)”. Section 241(a), (b) of , set out as a note preceding , Armed Forces, does not amend this section. Section 261(a)(1) of S. 2297, 116th Congress, known as the Coast Guard Authorization Act of 2019, as reported to the Senate, would have amended subsec. (d)(1) of this title by striking “reporting” and inserting “briefing”. S. 2297 was not enacted into law.Section 8513(a)(2)(B) of Pub. L. 116–283Pub. L. 116–283section 501 of Title 10

L. 116–283, § 8513(a)(2)(A)Subsec. (d)(1). , substituted “operations and support” for “operating expenses”.Pub. L. 116–283, § 8513(a)(2)(B)2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Not later than 90 days after the end of each fiscal year, the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives a report on each project undertaken during the course of the preceding fiscal year for which the amount expended under paragraph (1) exceeded $500,000.”Pub. children.label: a
children.text: Funds appropriated to or for the use of the Coast Guard for procurement, construction, and improvement of facilities and for research and development shall remain available until expended.

children.label: b
children.text: The Secretary may use any funds appropriated to or for the use of the Coast Guard for other construction purposes to restore, repair, or replace facilities that have been damaged or destroyed, including acquisition of sites.

children.label: c
children.text: The Secretary may use any funds appropriated to or for the use of the Coast Guard for other construction purposes to acquire, construct, convert, extend, and install at Coast Guard installations and facilities, needed permanent or temporary public works, including the preparation of sites and the furnishing of appurtenances, utilities, and equipment, but excluding the construction of family quarters, costing not more than $200,000 for any one project.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Subject to the reporting requirements set forth in paragraph (2), each fiscal year the Secretary may expend from amounts made available for the operations and support of the Coast Guard not more than $2,000,000 for minor construction and improvement projects at any location.

children.heading: .—Report
children.label: 2 .—Report
children.text: Not later than the date on which the President submits to Congress a budget under each year, the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate a report describing each project carried out under paragraph (1), in the most recently concluded fiscal year, for which the amount expended under such paragraph for such project was more than $1,000,000. If no such project was carried out during a fiscal year, no report under this paragraph shall be required with respect to that fiscal year.section 1105 of title 31
